name: Acids & Corrosives
notes: Table 2-3a. Acids are rated by general strength. Splash gives the Acid critical when splashed; Immersion gives the critical/hits delivered per round when immersed; Ingestion gives the attack level and the hit/round penalty per point of RR failure when a dose (a cupful for a Medium creature) is swallowed. Cost is silver pieces per cup (a lock-eating vial is 1/4 cost; ingredients are 1/10). Breakage Mod. modifies the breakage roll when eating through a lock/object. Diff. is the Crafting: Culinary difficulty to brew. A single cup up to Weak may be brewed in a kitchen or field; stronger or larger batches need a chemical workshop. Any Absolute Failure while brewing splashes the brewer.

name: Biome Codes
notes: Table 2-3b. The letter codes used in the herb, antidote, intoxicant, and poison tables to indicate where a substance may be found. They match Core Law Table 15-1. A code of /A indicates a high-altitude (alpine) variant of the other listed biome(s) — e.g., F/A is high-altitude temperate forest.

name: Antidotes
notes: Table 2-3c. Find is the difficulty to locate the herb; Cost is in silver pieces per dose. Unless otherwise indicated, the effects take 1-10 minutes. Prepared with Herbalism. See Biome Codes for letter codes.
rows: Level 4 antidote to muscle poisons. Flowers found 1-3 weeks after rain.
Level 9 antidote for reduction poisons. Reverses the effect of the curse Ugliness of Orn. Coastal flower.
Level 7 antidote for circulatory poisons. Seed cones.
Level 3 antidote for respiratory poisons. Berries from a black-stemmed vine.
Level 4 antidote for nerve poisons. Riparian shrub.
Level 20 antidote for Nur-oiolosse (see Life Preservation Herbs).
Level 50 antidote for any poison. Must be taken within 1 day of poisoning.
Level 20 antidote for nerve venoms. Flowering shrub.

name: General Purpose Herbs
notes: Table 2-3d. Find is the difficulty to locate the herb; Cost is in silver pieces per dose. Unless otherwise indicated, the effects take 1-10 rounds. Prepared with Herbalism. See Biome Codes for letter codes.
rows: Heals 2-12 hits and adds +10 to recovery rolls, not cumulative with other herbs.
Decongestant. +20 to resistance vs. common cold. Speeds recovery from respiratory illness by 5x.
Cures fever. Found on river rocks.
Preservation of organic material for 1 day as fresh, halves rate of decay for another week.
Repels any insect (level 20 effect). Smells foul (noticeably at 50').
Mental summons of one "friend" (beast or folk). Range is 300' x user's level. Also allows recovery from coma in 1-4 days. Coastal plant.
+10 to disease resistance for 1-10 days, cures common cold. Heals 1-2 hits. Riparian flower.
Preservation of any organic material for 1-10 weeks.
+10 to disease resistance for 1-10 days. Found near rivers, streams, and lakes.
Provides one day's nutrition. Will keep for two years in cool weather.

name: Injury Treatment Herbs
notes: Table 2-3e. A magical healing herb treating an injury grants +25 to the Medicine maneuver and +50 to the recovery-time roll (Core Law Table 13-1a); combined with a healing spell the bonus to the recovery roll is +75. Herbs which restore hits take 1-10 rounds to do so. Find is the difficulty to locate the herb; Cost is in silver pieces per dose. Prepared with Herbalism. See Biome Codes for letter codes.
rows: Coastal grass. Treats burns, preventing scarring.
Treats burns, skin irritation. May heal 5 hits from burns or minor cuts.
Stops bleeding by clotting and sealing wound. Takes 3 rounds to take effect. Patient cannot move for one hour or wound will reopen.
Treats up to moderate fractures. Riparian flower.
Treats sprains, torn ligaments, and cartilage damage.
Saltwater reed. Treats fractures of all severity. Ensures no permanent damage.
Treats any eye damage, including loss of an eye.
Treats fractures of all severity. Prevents infection of injury.
Treats sprains. Coastal flower; may also grow in salty soil.
Treats fractures of all severity. Grows on river rocks.
Treats any hearing damage. Riparian grass.
Stops any bleeding. Takes 1-10 rounds to take effect. Patient cannot move for one hour or wound will reopen.
Immediately stops any form of bleeding. Treats cut wound.
Immediately stops any form of bleeding. Treats cut wound.
Treats frostbite. Heals 2-20 hits from cold.
Treats light-moderate burns. Heals 1-10 hits from heat.
Treats any organ damage, up to and including destroyed organs. User is sensitive to the sun (-20 and 3 hits of damage per round of full sun) for 1-3 weeks.
Treats organ damage. Nausea for 1-10 hours (-50).
Prevents permanent damage in treated injury.
Treats frostbite. Heals 3-30 hits from cold. Tropical coastal sea plant.

name: Life Preservation Herbs
notes: Table 2-3f. Find is the difficulty to locate the herb; Cost is in silver pieces per dose. Unless otherwise indicated, the effects take 1-10 rounds. Prepared with Herbalism. See Biome Codes for letter codes.
rows: Removes up to -10 in Life Drain penalties. No effect for additional doses given within the next month.
Lifegiving for Elves, if given within 28 days of death.
Lifegiving if given within 1 day of death. Kills one day later unless Sorel nut is ingested. Found near waterfalls or rapids.
Lifegiving for Elves if given within 7 days of death. Found in shallow fresh water.
Lifegiving, if given within 56 days of death. Only found above 3200' elevation.
Lifegiving, if given within 30 days of death.

name: Minor Healing Herbs
notes: Table 2-3g. Effects are immediate. Find is the difficulty to locate the herb; Cost is in silver pieces per dose. Prepared with Herbalism. See Biome Codes for letter codes.
rows: Heals 1-5 hits. Found only above 2000' elevation.
Heals 1-10 hits on each of two consecutive rounds. Coastal weed.
Heals 10 hits. Instant effect. Found only above 3000' elevation.
Heals 100 hits. Found only near volcanoes or hot springs.
Heals 10 hits. Instant effect. Found only above 1500'.
Heals 50 hits. Found only above 2200' elevation.

name: Physical Alteration & Enhancement Herbs
notes: Table 2-3h. Find is the difficulty to locate the herb; Cost is in silver pieces per dose. Unless otherwise indicated, the effects take 1-10 rounds. AF is the Addiction Factor for those that are addictive. Prepared with Herbalism. See Biome Codes for letter codes.
rows: Greatly reduces body's oxygen consumption for 12 hours. Can breathe water (lungs will fill with water, though). No penalties to Endurance for altitude. Use more often than every other day causes E Impact critical (no RR or size adjustment).
Restores as full night's sleep. AF 9.
Protects eyes in intense light or glare. Allows sight despite sudden or blinding light. Lasts 9 hours.
Enhanced vision (triple range, +25 Perception) for 3 hours.
Subtracts 30 from maneuvers and melee. Adds 50 to SCRs and ranged attacks. Euphoria. Lasts 1 hour. AF 7. Found around volcanoes active within the past century.
Heart stimulant. Instant effect. Doubles speed for 1 round (+4 AP). Using more than once in an hour requires a Physical RR (level 25). Failure indicates heart stoppage. AF 15.
Intoxication and dreams (actions at -50) for two hours. Provides one day's nutrition. AF 18.
Coastal salt water algae. Allows breathing underwater (only) for 4 hours. Lungs will clear at end of this time automatically.
Allows mental summons of one known sentient friend (up to half a mile away).
Increases strength for 1-10 minutes. +10 Strength bonus. AF 12.
Protects completely against natural flame and heat for 1-10 hours.
Increases visual perception (double range, +25 perception) for 10 minutes. Found above 3100'.
Bonus of +20 to SCRs. Lasts 1 hour. Maneuver and melee penalty of -20. 10% chance any targeted spell will be cast on nearest unintended target. AF 5.
For "good in heart," instills confidence and singleness of purpose (+25 to all actions related to that purpose, -10 for unrelated actions) for 1-4 hours.
Acute smell and taste (+50 perception) for 1 hour. AF 3.
Haste: +4 AP for three rounds. Instant effect. AF 22.
Enhances smell and hearing (triple range; +50 perception). Lasts 1 hour. AF 8.

name: Stun Relief Herbs
notes: Table 2-3i. Effects are immediate. Find is the difficulty to locate the herb; Cost is in silver pieces per dose. Prepared with Herbalism. See Biome Codes for letter codes.

name: Intoxicants
notes: Table 2-3j. Substances taken for the subjective experience of the user; most are addictive. Each use, the GM rolls d100 + AF (Addiction Factor); over 100 means a level 1 addiction (raising by one each subsequent failed roll). Rarity is the difficulty to find; Cost is in silver pieces per dose. Prepared with Herbalism or Poison Mastery (alcohol uses Crafting: Culinary). See Biome Codes for letter codes.
rows: Causes sleep and quick unconsciousness. One hour's sleep equals 4.
Euphoric. Allows for shared dreams with family member who is also sleeping. Found only near active volcanoes. Available late winter-early summer.
Leaf produces smoke which affects 20' radius. Relaxes for 1-10 rounds (-75 to all actions).
Euphoric hallucinogen. +10 to friendly social interactions for 2 hours, but afterwards user is weak 1-10 hours (-50 to all actions).
Hallucinogen. Cures mind loss and mental diseases, but prevents all movement for 1-10 weeks. Available in spring-early summer.
Causes sleep and quick unconsciousness. One hour's sleep equals six. Coastal vine.
Euphoria for 1 hour (all actions at -50). Gives one day's nutrition. Found in volcanic areas.
Relaxant. User takes double usual hits before passing out. All actions are at -30. Lasts 1-2 hours. Available early-mid summer.
Invisible smoke relaxes (-75 to all actions) for 1-10 rounds.
Euphoric. Allows for shared dreams with friend similarly affected.
Addiction checks should only be made upon reaching a "drunken" state (i.e., drinking with moderation does not cause addiction).

Addiction checks should only be made after chewing or inhaling the smoke from more than three ounces of tobacco. However, once addicted, the AF becomes equal to the addiction level. Harvest is late summer-early autumn, but dries well.

name: Poisons & Irritants
notes: Table 2-3k. The Type column gives the poison category (general effects per Core Law Section 15.6); the listed effects are specific and take priority over general effects in a conflict. Unless otherwise noted, irritants allow a Physical RR to resist. Rarity is the difficulty to find; Cost is in silver pieces per dose. Prepared with Poison Mastery (increase preparation difficulty by one category if Herbalism is used instead). See Biome Codes for letter codes.
rows: Level 1. Reddish juice causes massive blood clotting. Available 1-3 weeks after rain falls.
Level 6. Black powder does 10-100 hits immediately. Maximum of moderate general effects.
Level 7. Reddish paste causes heart to malfunction. All effects begin in 2-12 rounds.
Level 5. Golden paste causes 1-10 day coma. This effect begins after all general effects have begun.
Level 10. Brown paste does 1-10 hits immediately. Maximum of mild effects.
Level 3. Brown liquid slows blood flow and victim needs double usual sleep until fully recovered. Maximum of moderate general effects. Available late summer through autumn.
Level 5. Bluish juice causes victim's body to heat up. Severe effects result in spontaneous combustion. Comes from a phosphorescent green firefly.
Level 2. Black venom of arctic fish causes hardening of tendons in 1-4 appendages. Severe effects make appendages useless.
Level 6. Yellow venom acts on victim's eyes. Severe effects converts optic juices to honey.
Level 2. Orange tree sap affects mucous membranes. Severe effect turns mucous to acid (blinds, or destroys windpipe and/or esophagus).
Level 2. Green venom acts on appendages. Severe effects turn 1-4 appendages to stone.
Level 8. Brownish powder sterilizes and does 5-50 hits immediately. Maximum of moderate general effects.
Level 9. This powder causes severe itching on contact. Victim is at -30 to all actions for 1 hour per 5 failure of RR. Takes effect in 3-30 rounds.
Level 7. This powder causes blinding when it gets in the eyes, lasting 1 minute for each 1 failure on the RR.
Level 12. This juice, when drunk, induces vomiting. +50 RR to swallowed poisons if taken within 15 minutes. Immediate XH (-30) Exhaustion check, -15 to all actions for 10 minutes.
Level 3. Coastal shrub provides a tan paste that affects hearing and does 1-10 hits immediately. Maximum of severe general effects (destroys hearing).
Level 1. Affects 1-4 appendages. Maximum of severe general effects (loss of 1-10 temporary Agility). Available mid- to late spring.
Level 4. Acts in 1-10 rounds. Mild effect is blindness in 1-2 eyes. Moderate effect is complete blindness and coma. Severe effect is death.
Level 10. Black paste affects nervous system. Extreme effect destroys nervous system and kills instantly. Available in spring and early summer. Found 1500' or higher.
Level 3. Brownish-red snake venom. Maximum of moderate effects (upper body paralysis).
Level 1. White powder instantly causes mild euphoria (victim at -50 for 1-10 rounds). Extreme effect is that brain is destroyed after 1-10 minutes. AF 1. Available late summer and autumn.
Level 2. Maximum of severe effect (insanity in 1-100 weeks).
Level 4. Brownish cave-drake saliva. Maximum of severe effects (erosion of nervous system in 1-20 rounds; victim loses 2-4 AP each round).
Level 2. Acts in 1-2 rounds. Maximum of moderate effects (victim loses feeling in 1-6 extremities [heads, genitals, hands, feet] for 1-100 days).
Level 20. Extreme effects result in destruction of nervous system (paralyzed instantly; dead in 1-10 minutes). Blossoms in spring/early summer. Found above 2300'.

Level 5. Tropical fish toxin acts in 1-100 rounds. Mild effect is deep sleep. Moderate effect is degeneration of balance and hearing (-50 to relevant maneuvers). Severe effect is total paralysis (this is the maximum effect).
Level 10. Grey/black blood affects exposed areas. Maximum of severe effects (dissolves afflicted area in 1-10 rounds). Affects metals and organic material. Does not affect glass, sand, or pottery.
Level 3. Milky white juices affect organs. Maximum of severe effects (liquefies one organ in 1-10 minutes).
Level 3. Blue/grey paste causes dehydration. Extreme effect is fatal in 1-10 minutes.
Level 9. Silver powder scars skin; gives 2-20 hits immediately. Maximum of moderate effects.
Level 5. Extreme effect is slow (1 day) paralysis and death (1-10 days).
Level 5. Bluish extract. Maximum of severe effects (blinds by reducing optic nerve to water); does 1-10 hits.
Level 9. Green powder. Maximum of severe effects (except for elves, who can suffer extreme effects).

Level 4. Mist/vapor from tree acts as depressant causing immediate and continuing sleep (5 minutes per 1 failure; new RR is made when time elapses if mist is still present). There are no other effects. AF 2.
Level 7. Brown paste does 1-100 hits immediately. Maximum of mild effects.
Level 5. Yellow venom does 5-50 hits immediately. Maximum of mild effects.
Level 2. Tan paste. Extreme effect destroys lungs and kills in 1-100 rounds.
Level 3. Brown juice does 3-300 hits immediately. Maximum of mild effects. Available mid-summer through early autumn.
Level 6. Creamy paste does 3-30 hits immediately. Maximum of mild effects.
Level 2. Pinkish paste delivers 1-100 hits immediately. Maximum of mild effects. Available in late spring to early summer.
